---
# Processes targeting lsass (credential-access seed)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

A coarse credential-access seed: PROCESS events whose TARGET (`action_process_image_name`) is `lsass.exe`, grouped by the acting process. NOTE: true LSASS memory access (T1003.001) is usually NOT a process-spawn — XDR surfaces handle/EPM access events for that — so treat this as a starting filter and pivot to the EPM/handle telemetry for the real dump detection. Shown here to illustrate the actor-vs-action target distinction.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and action_process_image_name = "lsass.exe"
| comp count() as c by actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_name
| sort desc c
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (narrow 30-min window); measured ~0.0005 CU. Narrow the time window + filter early to keep real-dataset hunts cheap._
